title: Excretory & Nervous System – Page 416
section: Biology: Excretory System, Nervous System & Brain

questions.question: Lymphocytes present in lymph help to prevent the body from diseases by:

options.B: Killing harmful bacteria and other toxic foreign bodies

questions.explanation: Page 416: Lymphocytes in lymph prevent diseases by killing harmful bacteria and toxic foreign bodies.

questions.question: The node found in lymph vessels is called:
questions.explanation: Page 416: The node found in lymph vessels is called lymph node. It works as a filter of microbes.

questions.question: Lymph helps in:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Lymph helps in healing the wounds.

questions.question: Excretion is the removal of:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Excretion is removal of waste products formed during metabolism from the body.

questions.question: The excretory organs of human are:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Excretory organs: 1. Kidneys, 2. Skin, 3. Liver, 4. Lungs.

questions.question: The main excretory organ in human is:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Kidneys are the main excretory organ in human and other mammals. Weight is 140 grams.

questions.question: Each kidney is made up of approximately how many kidney ducts (nephrons)?
questions.explanation: Page 416: Each kidney is made up of approximately 1,30,00,000 kidney ducts called nephrons.

questions.question: Nephron is the structural and functional unit of:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Nephron is the structural and functional unit of the kidney.

questions.question: The cup-like structure in every nephron is called:
questions.explanation: Page 416: There is a cup-like structure in every nephron called Bowman's capsule.

questions.question: Glomerulus is made up of:

options.B: Thin blood vessels found in Bowman's capsule

questions.explanation: Page 416: Glomerulus is made up of thin blood vessels found in Bowman's capsule.

questions.question: The process of filtration of liquid into cavity of Bowman's capsule is called:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Process of filtration of liquid into cavity of Bowman's capsule is called ultra filtration.

questions.question: The main function of the kidneys is:

options.B: Purification of blood plasma by excreting nitrogenous waste through urination

questions.explanation: Page 416: Main function of kidneys is purification of blood plasma, excreting unwanted nitrogenous waste substances through urination.

questions.question: In kidneys, average blood filtrated per minute is:
questions.explanation: Page 416: In kidneys, average 125 mL per minute blood is filtrated, i.e., 180 litres per day.

questions.question: How many litres of urine is formed daily?
questions.explanation: Page 416: Out of 180 litres filtered, 1.45 litres urine is formed daily and remaining is absorbed back.

questions.question: Urine is an aqueous solution in which water constitutes:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Urine is an aqueous solution in which 95% is water. Other constituents include urea, uric acid, chloride, sodium, potassium, creatinine.

questions.question: The colour of urine is light yellow due to presence of:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Colour of urine is light yellow due to presence of urochromes, formed by dissociation of haemoglobin.

questions.question: The correct sequence of urine flow in human body is:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Correct sequence: Kidney → Ureter → Urinary bladder → Urethra.

questions.question: Urine pH is:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Urine is acidic. Its pH value is 6.

questions.question: Kidney stones are made up of:
questions.explanation: Page 416: The stone formed in kidneys is made up of calcium oxalate.

questions.question: Excretory parts of Arthropods are:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Arthropods excrete by Malpighian tubules.

questions.question: Excretory parts of Flat worm are:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Flat worm excretory parts are flame cells.

questions.question: Excretory parts of Animals of Annelida Phylum are:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Annelida Phylum excretory parts are Nephridia.

questions.question: Hemodialysis is the process of:

options.B: Removal of excess urea from blood using artificial kidney

questions.explanation: Page 416: Hemodialysis is removal of excess urea from the blood of patient using artificial kidney.

questions.question: Excretory parts of Curstaceans (crabs, lobsters) are:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Curstaceans excretory parts are Antennal gland.

questions.question: Liver cells play the main role in excretion by converting amino acids and ammonia into:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Liver cells play main role in excretion by converting amino acids and ammonia of blood into urea.

questions.question: Lungs excrete which gaseous substances?
questions.explanation: Page 416: Lungs excrete carbon dioxide and water vapour.

questions.question: Nervous System of human is divided into how many parts?
questions.explanation: Page 416: Nervous System is divided into 3 parts: 1. CNS, 2. PNS, 3. Autonomic Nervous System.

questions.question: Central Nervous System is made up of:
questions.explanation: Page 416: CNS of human is made up of two parts - Brain and Spinal Cord.

questions.question: Brain is covered by a membrane called:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Brain is covered by membrane called meninges. It is situated in a bony box called cranium.

questions.question: Brain is situated in a bony box called:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Brain is situated in a bony box called cranium which protects it from external injury.

questions.question: Brain is divided into three parts:

options.B: Fore brain, Mid brain, Hind brain

questions.explanation: Page 416: Brain is divided into Fore brain, Mid brain, and Hind brain.

questions.question: The weight of the human brain is:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Weight of the brain of human is 1336 grams.

questions.question: Fore brain includes:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Fore brain includes Cerebrum, Thalamus and Hypothalamus.

questions.question: Cerebrum is the centre of:

options.B: Wisdom, will power, memory, knowledge and thinking

questions.explanation: Page 416: Cerebrum is the most developed part of brain. Centre of wisdom, will power, movements, memory, knowledge and thinking.

questions.question: Cerebrum is the largest part of the brain and is responsible for:

options.B: Analysis, coordination of muscular movements from sense organs

questions.explanation: Page 416: Cerebrum is the largest part. It analyses and coordinates muscular movements received from sense organs.

questions.question: The function of Thalamus is:

options.B: It is the centre of feeling pain, cold and heat

questions.explanation: Page 416: Thalamus is the centre of feeling pain, cold and heat.

questions.question: Hind brain consists of:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Hind brain consists of Cerebellum, Pons and Medulla.

questions.question: Outer part of kidney is called:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Outer part is called cortex and inner part is called medulla.

questions.question: Which arteriole carries blood TO the glomerulus?
questions.explanation: Page 416: Afferent arteriole carries blood to the glomerulus. Efferent arteriole takes blood out.

questions.question: Blood supply to the kidneys compared to other organs is:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Supply of blood to kidneys takes place in large quantity in comparison to other organs.

questions.question: Unicellular animals excrete by:

options.B: Diffusion through general body surface and contractile vacuole

questions.explanation: Page 416: Unicellular animals excrete by diffusion through general body surface and contractile vacuole.

questions.question: Animals of Porifera Phylum excrete by:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Porifera Phylum animals excrete by general body surface.

questions.question: Coelenterates excrete:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Coelenterates excrete directly by cells.

questions.question: Mollusca excrete by:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Mollusca excrete by urinary organ (by urinary organ/kidney-like structure).

questions.question: Vertebrates mainly excrete by:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Vertebrates mainly excrete by kidneys.

questions.question: Oil gland and sweat glands found in skin are part of:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Skin has oil glands and sweat glands. Skin is the second excretory organ.

questions.question: The excretion of some spices like garlic, onion happens through:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Lungs excrete vapour component of some substances like garlic, onion and some spices.

questions.question: Input and output nerves meet at:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Input and output nerves meet at central nervous system.

questions.question: Mid brain contains:
questions.explanation: Page 416: Mid brain contains Corpora quadrigemina.

questions.question: Lymph helps in transportation of:

options.B: Different materials like oxygen, protein, and nutrients to tissues

questions.explanation: Page 416: Lymph helps in transportation of different materials like oxygen, protein and other nutrients to the tissues.
